channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Trump signs executive orders to shrink two national monuments in Utah, potentially opening them up to oil and mineral exploration
item.description: The orders reduce the size of Grand Staircase-Escalante National Monument from about 1.87 million acres to about 181,500 acres, and Bears Ears National monument from 1.36 million acres to approximately 121,100 acres. The president has declared a national energy emergency and rolled back dozens of environmental protections, seeking to aggressively boost drilling for oil and gas and mining for critical minerals that are essential to energy infrastructure.

item.title: Eating this food regularly could lead to greater longevity, a doctor writes
item.description: Columnist Trisha Pasricha explains that your heart, gut — and even your lifespan — may benefit from spicy foods.

item.title: Lindsey Graham’s sister is picked to serve the rest of his Senate term
item.description: South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster (R) announced Darline Graham Nordone’s appointment, hours after President Donald Trump publicly called for her to fill the vacancy.

item.title: Trump’s sons invest heavily in defense tech as their father’s administration pours money in, a Washington Post analysis finds
item.description: Most investments by funds linked to Donald Trump Jr. and Eric Trump have taken place since their father’s 2024 election.

item.title: The saga of Slurpee Park: How D.C. failed to name a patch of public land
item.description: Lawmakers voted 20 years ago to name a pocket park for an Adams Morgan activist. Here’s why it never happened.

item.title: Trump’s personal attorneys and DOJ leaders misused courts in case that spawned plan for $1.8B fund, judge says
item.description: President Donald Trump’s lawsuit against the IRS — which resulted in the now-scuttled proposal for the payout fund — was filed for an “improper purpose,” U.S. District Judge Kathleen M. Williams said in a ruling Monday. She referred the attorneys involved for potential disciplinary proceedings.

item.title: Federal officers involved in deadly shooting in Maine, governor says
item.description: Federal and state officials were seeking to determine what occurred, said Maine Gov. Janet Mills (D). Local police said personnel from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement were present during the shooting.

item.title: 12 states sue to block Paramount Skydance from buying Warner Bros.
item.description: The states’ Democratic attorneys general allege that the deal violates antitrust law and would harm competition. Warner Bros. shareholders and the Justice Department have already blessed the deal.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: ‘Careless and carefree’ wife is never on time, even for big events
item.description: They’ve been married a long time, but letter writer is “at a loss” over wife’s chronic tardiness.

item.title: Trump recommends Lindsey Graham’s sister to serve rest of his U.S. Senate term
item.description: The president recommended that South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster select Darline Graham Nordone to serve until January. McMaster has scheduled an announcement for Monday.

item.title: Trump says U.S. military is reimposing blockade on Iranian ports, significantly escalating conflict
item.description: President Donald Trump’s decision came after days of renewed bombing. The move takes away the last major concession the United States had made to Tehran that had helped foster a ceasefire in the conflict.

item.title: Compare your monthly fuel costs for an EV versus a gas car
item.description: Gas prices are still elevated, making the gap between fueling a gas car and an electric vehicle more striking.

item.title: The testosterone craze fuels fear — not masculinity
item.description: Here’s the truth about testosterone levels and health.

item.title: Start your week off with today’s On the Record
item.description: Start your week off with today’s On the Record

item.title: Lindsey Graham. Mitch McConnell. Trump’s sons. Algae contractor. Worker shortage. EV costs. Nighttime urination. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Why recruiters can’t find workers and new grads can’t find jobs (it’s not AI)
item.description: Experts say a major labor shortage looms because of population shifts and a mismatch between new graduates’ skills and employers’ needs.

item.title: Sen. Mitch McConnell says he was hospitalized because of a fall and contracted pneumonia, after weeks of questions about his health
item.description: The Kentucky Republican provided the most detailed update on his health since he was hospitalized nearly a month ago, telling constituents on Sunday that he is not healthy enough yet to return to the Senate.

item.title: Lindsey Graham’s office says he died of ‘aortic dissection’ due to cardiovascular disease, citing medical examiner’s early findings
item.description: A death certificate remains pending until all toxicological and microscopic testing is finalized, the senator’s office said.

item.title: He fleeced the Navy for millions, then fled the country. Now, Fat Leonard says he wants a pardon.
item.description: In an exclusive interview, legendary con man Leonard Glenn Francis, back in prison, tells The Washington Post about his wild escape and his bid for a presidential pardon.

item.title: What happens next with Lindsey Graham’s Senate seat, and what the Republican’s death means for Trump’s agenda in Congress
item.description: South Carolina’s Republican governor can appoint a replacement, followed by a special election that is likely to be highly competitive.

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham, who went from Trump critic to close ally, was a steadfast foreign policy hawk over three decades in Congress
item.description: The South Carolina Republican’s remarkable political trajectory included election to the House in the 1994 GOP takeover, a role as an impeachment manager against President Bill Clinton, a Senate career featuring bipartisan collegiality, and a position as a confidant of President Donald Trump who epitomized Trump’s capture of the Republican Party.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: She and dad bond over baseball, but sister calls it unfair
item.description: Letter writer connects with 82-year-old dad through baseball. Why is her sister so adamant that going to games is a bad idea?

item.title: Before Reflecting Pool, company hired to clean up algae had troubled project on trash-infested river
item.description: Greenwater Services, owned by a donor to President Donald Trump, has received two no-bid contracts from the government since Trump returned to office.

item.title: Meghan McCain on Lindsey Graham: The last of the ‘Three Amigos’ is gone
item.description: Meghan McCain on Lindsey Graham: The last of the ‘Three Amigos’ is gone

item.title: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
item.description: Play The Washington Post's daily crossword puzzles online for free.

item.title: Survivors of Iranian attack that killed 6 U.S. troops say generals ignored warnings
item.description: The drone strike on Port Shuaiba in Kuwait occurred on day two of the war. Those who were there say they fear no one will be held accountable.

item.title: Lindsey Graham, longtime Republican senator from South Carolina, dies after sudden illness, his office said. He was 71.
item.description: Graham's office said the senator died of “a brief and sudden illness” on Saturday night.

item.title: U.S. carries out strikes on Iran in response to attack on cargo ship, as Tehran declares it is closing the Strait of Hormuz again
item.description: U.S. Central Command said Saturday it was carrying out a new round of strikes against Iran after the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps fired on a vessel attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz. Tehran claimed that it had fired a warning shot at a ship attempting to cross what it deemed to be an unapproved route.

item.title: How to reduce nighttime trips to the bathroom, according to doctors
item.description: Simple lifestyle changes can often help if nighttime urination is affecting your quality of life.

item.title: Unexplained zombielike spells haunted him for years. A fall revealed an unusual diagnosis.
item.description: The deacon’s stumbling spells were becoming more frequent when blood tests yielded a startling result.

item.title: Justice Dept. subpoenas N.Y. Times journalists over reports on new Air Force One, in latest example of unusually aggressive move
item.description: The subpoenas were related to recent coverage of President Donald Trump’s new Qatari-donated Air Force One plane, which the Times reported lacked some of the security features of his previous aircraft, including antimissile capabilities, a spokesperson for the paper confirmed.

item.title: A reader asks: Am I having too much sex? Our new column offers relationship advice.
item.description: “The searching and the messaging and the dating” takes up all of this letter writer’s time. Is that so bad?

item.title: Democrats are growing bullish on retaking the Senate. Here are the races to watch.
item.description: The unpredictable Maine and Michigan Senate races remain critical to controlling the chamber.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: ‘Teen takeovers’ confound cities struggling to define the problem — and find solutions
item.description: A spate of sometimes violent gatherings has stoked demands for action. But officials know they need to provide outlets for the many teens who are not breaking laws.

item.title: In Rome, a new tourist trap emerges: The pasta-making nonna
item.description: The women making fresh pasta in restaurant windows are a hit with tourists. Locals say it’s all wrong.

item.title: Graham Platner officially withdraws from Maine Senate race, keeping his name off the ballot
item.description: Maine’s secretary of state confirmed Friday that the Democratic candidate had filed the necessary paperwork. Platner’s exit comes after he was accused of sexual assault by a woman he previously dated.

item.title: Somali school in Minnesota faces threats after Trump posts clip of kindergartners
item.description: The president’s post clearly showed the students’ faces. Replies filled with anti-Muslim invectives, including one user who labeled the children “future terrorists.”

item.title: 10 tips from Dr. Leana S. Wen to improve your health while aging
item.description: Decline in old age isn’t inevitable. Here’s what older adults can do to prevent it.

item.title: Heat dome to topple records in drought-stricken Plains and Western states
item.description: See where temperatures could reach a rare 110 degrees and amplify fire weather risks.

item.title: Carolyn Hax chat: Is daughter wasting her life as her grandma’s caretaker?
item.description: Carolyn Hax chat: Is daughter wasting her life as her grandma's caretaker?

item.title: What ‘Little House on the Prairie’ got wrong about America: Columnist Monica Hesse on the beloved books and the new adaptation
item.description: The Netflix series based on Laura Ingalls Wilder’s books takes us to the open frontier of our national imagination.

item.title: Assigning FEMA staff to immigration enforcement hurt disaster work, House Democrats’ report says
item.description: The findings by a group of House Democrats show how FEMA staffers were put to work in immigration enforcement and administrative roles, among others, straining the agency’s disaster response ability.

item.title: The U.S. will pay for the chain reaction Trump set off in NATO, Fareed Zakaria writes
item.description: A Europe that spends more on defense will be less dependent — and less deferential.

item.title: We don't know what food is spreading the parasite giving thousands of people diarrhea. Here’s how to lower your risk.
item.description: There’s a lag between when people consume the parasite and when symptoms appear, making it tough for those infected to remember what they ate to pinpoint the problem.

item.title: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record
item.description: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record.

item.title: ICE shooting. Trump’s lawyers. Maine race. Triumphal arch. El Niño. Mystery parasite. Pasta nonnas. Catch up now.
item.description: The 7.

item.title: Migrant passengers who saw man killed by ICE in Houston say he did not ram federal officers with his vehicle
item.description: In separate statements and interviews, the three men disputed key elements of the Department of Homeland Security’s account of the killing of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo during a chaotic traffic stop this week. They spoke from immigration detention with attorney Hugo Balderas-Ibarra, who shared their written and oral accounts with The Washington Post.

item.title: This newly defined condition affects roughly 90 percent of U.S. adults
item.description: Fresh guidelines may soon make the disorder known as CKM syndrome a common diagnosis.

item.title: Trump’s proposed triumphal arch clears another key hurdle
item.description: The National Capital Planning Commission, led by the president’s allies, voted to advance the project near Arlington Cemetery, tabling a height question for later.
